case_title: MAPLE MULTI-FAMILY LAND CA, L.P., VS. TERRACON CONSULTANTS, INC., ET AL

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Referee For All Purposes
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Erin Leigh Dake, Esq. 408-292-1765 of Miller Morton Caillat & Nevis LLP for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Frank Perrott, Esq. 916-564-5400 of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Hearing is held. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MAPLE MULTI-FAMILY LAND CA, L.P.'S Motion For Order Appointing Referee For All Purposes.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

***After the hearing, the Court signs an Order Denying Motion for Referee Appointment.(see scanned order for details)***

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Referee For All Purposes
Off Calendar Sep-20-2019 Continued To Sep-27-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Erin Leigh Dake, Esq. 408-292-1765 of Miller Morton Caillat & Nevis LLP for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court informs Counsel it will not sign any order and is continuing the motion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MAPLE MULTI-FAMILY LAND CA, L.P.'S Motion For Order Appointing Referee For All Purposes.

The hearing of this matter is continued to September 27.

Plaintiff Maple Multi-Family Land CA, L.P. filed and served a motion on August 21, 2019, for an order appointing a referee for all purposes. Without excuse or explanation, defendant Terracon Consultants, Inc. filed a tardy opposition on September 19 - seven court days after it was due and two court days after a reply from Maple would have been due. The court desires a non-hurried reply from Maple, so the hearing of this matter is continued to September 27. Maple's reply - it may be of any length - is due on September 25. For their bad-faith actions, Terracon and its counsel are ordered to pay the reasonable expenses, including attorney fees, incurred by Maple to draft, file and argue the reply brief. (CCP 128.5.)

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
